title: bromus squarrosus subsp. villosus
intro: Bromus squarrosus subsp. villosus, also known as the hairy-brook brome, is a subspecies of the field brome plant. It is a perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The hairy-brook brome has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
